note: Each run opens a real PR and may post to Slack. Use a throwaway branch and a test channel.

evals.prompt: open a PR for this branch (repo has a PR template and a project board)
evals.expected_output: PR body authored to a file filling every template section including a QA verification steps list and Closes #<N>; created as a DRAFT by a subagent with labels/milestone/assignee mirrored from the issue; PR added to the board at In Progress; PR URL and item id reported back.

evals.prompt: mark PR #<N> ready and announce it (Slack configured with a default reviewer)
evals.expected_output: Ready flipped only because checks passed and (if UI) media was posted; board moved to In Review; Slack message has the reviewer @mention on its own leading line and every link in <url|text> markup, no bare URLs.

evals.prompt: announce PR #<N> (still a draft, checks failing)
evals.expected_output: Refuses to announce: PR stays a draft, board stays In Progress, and the blocking reasons are stated explicitly.

evals.prompt: PR #<N> just got approved and merged — close it out
evals.expected_output: Merge state verified via gh pr view before posting. Final comment on the PR summarizing what actually shipped (including review-driven changes, checks/QA, deferred follow-ups); short comment on the linked issue referencing the PR with a one-line resolution. No board action taken (automation handles Done). Refuses if the PR is not actually merged.
